- [Film Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UK_Film_Council "UK Film Council") | | Distributed by | [Fox Searchlight Pictures](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fox_Searchlight_Pictures "Fox Searchlight Pictures") | | Release date | - 1 November 2002 (2002-11-01) (UK) | | Running time | 113 minutes[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-mojo-1) | | Country | United Kingdom | | Language | English | | Budget | $8 million[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-mojo-1) | | Box office | $82.8 million[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-2)[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-mojo2-3) | _**28 Days Later**_ (sometimes stylized with an [ellipsis](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ellipsis "Ellipsis") as _**28 Days Later...**_[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-bfi-4)) is a 2002 British [post-apocalyptic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apocalyptic_and_post-apocalyptic_fiction "Apocalyptic and post-apocalyptic fiction") [horror film](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film") directed by [Danny Boyle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Danny_Boyle "Danny Boyle") and written by [Alex Garland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alex_Garland "Alex Garland"). It stars [Cillian Murphy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cillian_Murphy "Cillian Murphy") as a bicycle courier who awakens from a [coma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coma "Coma") to discover that the accidental release of a highly contagious, aggression-inducing [virus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Virus "Virus") has caused the [breakdown of society](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Societal_collapse "Societal collapse"). [Naomie Harris](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naomie_Harris "Naomie Harris"), [Christopher Eccleston](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christopher_Eccleston "Christopher Eccleston"), [Megan Burns](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Megan_Burns "Megan Burns"), and [Brendan Gleeson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brendan_Gleeson "Brendan Gleeson") appear in supporting roles. Garland took inspiration from [George A. Romero](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_A._Romero "George A. Romero")'s [_Night of the Living Dead_ film series](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Night_of_the_Living_Dead_(film_series) "Night of the Living Dead (film series)") and [John Wyndham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Wyndham "John Wyndham")'s 1951 novel _[The Day of the Triffids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Day_of_the_Triffids "The Day of the Triffids")._ Filming took place in various locations in the United Kingdom in 2001. The crew filmed for brief periods during early mornings and temporarily closed streets in London to capture recognizable and typically busy areas when they were deserted. [John Murphy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Murphy_(composer) "John Murphy (composer)") composed [an original soundtrack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later:_The_Soundtrack_Album "28 Days Later: The Soundtrack Album") for the film, with other instrumental songs by [Brian Eno](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brian_Eno "Brian Eno"), [Godspeed You! Black Emperor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Godspeed_You!_Black_Emperor "Godspeed You! Black Emperor"), and other artists. _28 Days Later_ was released on 1 November 2002 in the United Kingdom by [Fox Searchlight Pictures](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fox_Searchlight_Pictures "Fox Searchlight Pictures"). It received generally positive reviews and was a commercial success. Grossing $82.8 million worldwide on a budget of $8 million, it became one of the most profitable horror films of 2002. Reviewers praised Boyle's direction, the cast's performances, Garland's screenplay, the atmosphere and soundtrack. Despite Boyle not considering it a [zombie film](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zombie_film "Zombie film"), _28 Days Later_ is credited with reinvigorating the genre and influencing a revival in it a decade after its release, with its fast-running infected and character-driven drama.[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-hollywoodreporter-5)[\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-Bloody-Disgusting-6) It has been featured in several "best-of" film lists. The film's success launched its [titular film series](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later_(film_series) "28 Days Later (film series)"), featuring three further installments: _[28 Weeks Later](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Weeks_Later "28 Weeks Later")_ (2007), _[28 Years Later](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Years_Later "28 Years Later")_ (2025), and _[28 Years Later: The Bone Temple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Years_Later:_The_Bone_Temple "28 Years Later: The Bone Temple")_ (2026). A wider franchise also includes the graphic novel _[28 Days Later: The Aftermath](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later:_The_Aftermath "28 Days Later: The Aftermath")_ (2007), and the comic book series _[28 Days Later](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later_(comics) "28 Days Later (comics)")_ (2009–2011). ## Plot \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=1 "Edit section: Plot")\] A group of animal rights activists infiltrate a [laboratory](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Huntingdon_Life_Sciences "Huntingdon Life Sciences") in [Cambridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cambridge "Cambridge") which houses abnormally aggressive [chimpanzees](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Chimpanzee "Chimpanzee"). Despite a scientist warning that the chimpanzees are "infected" with " [rage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rage_(emotion) "Rage (emotion)")", an activist frees one; it attacks her and, within seconds of exposure, she succumbs to the virus. She immediately attacks the others, and the virus spreads rapidly across [Great Britain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Great_Britain "Great Britain"), resulting in total [societal collapse](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Societal_collapse "Societal collapse"). Twenty-eight days later, bicycle courier Jim, who had a traffic accident and fell into a [coma](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coma "Coma") before the outbreak, wakes in [St Thomas' Hospital](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/St_Thomas%27_Hospital "St Thomas' Hospital") in [London](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/London "London") to find it deserted. Leaving the hospital, Jim wanders the empty streets of London, discovers newspapers headlining a mass evacuation, and eventually enters a church and witnesses the aftermath of a massacre, presumably caused by the infected people. An infected priest and members of the public appear and chase after Jim, but he is rescued by survivors Selena and Mark, who take him to their refuge in a [Tube station](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/London_Underground "London Underground") shop. At Jim's request, the group travels on foot to his parents' house in [Deptford](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Deptford "Deptford"), where he learns that they have died by [suicide](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suicide_pact "Suicide pact"), leaving him a note to "never wake up". When dusk settles in, the group decides to stay the night. Staying up, Jim reminisces over family memories and lights a candle, drawing the infected neighbours to the house. Mark is bitten while eliminating them, forcing Selena to kill him. Jim and Selena make their way up [Balfron Tower](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Balfron_Tower "Balfron Tower") after seeing a makeshift [signal lamp](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Signal_lamp "Signal lamp") in a flat belonging to [cab driver](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hackney_carriage "Hackney carriage") Frank and his young daughter Hannah. Frank plays them a military radio broadcast offering protection and "salvation from infection" at a blockade outside [Manchester](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Manchester "Manchester"). Frank plans to take Hannah to the blockade, and the four agree to travel together. Reaching Manchester, they find the blockade deserted. Frank, frustrated, kicks a gate on which a dead infected body is balanced. A drop of blood from the corpse falls into Frank's eye, infecting him. As he warns the others to keep their distance, hidden soldiers appear and shoot him dead as he turns. The soldiers bring Jim, Selena and Hannah to a heavily fortified country house under the command of Major Henry West, treat their guests to a tour and banquet and introduces him to "Private Mailer", an infected soldier kept chained up for observation. However, the sanctuary turns out to be a ruse; West reveals to Jim that his broadcast was intended to lure female survivors into [sexual slavery](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sexual_slavery "Sexual slavery") to maintain his troops' morale, giving them faith in a future where the human race survives. Jim and Sergeant Farrell refuse to go along with this scheme, so West orders them to be imprisoned. While chained, Farrell tells Jim that the infection has not spread across the Atlantic and that Britain has been quarantined from the rest of the world. Two soldiers shoot Farrell and then argue, allowing Jim to escape into the woods. Jim notices a jet [contrail](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Contrail "Contrail") in the sky, showing proof that the outside world is still active. After luring West away from the mansion with a siren, Jim returns and frees Private Mailer in order to infect and kill the remaining soldiers. One, Corporal Mitchell, attempts to abduct Selena, but Jim kills him. Jim, Selena, and Hannah attempt to escape in Frank's cab, but West, who has hidden in the back seat, shoots Jim in the stomach. Hannah retaliates by putting the cab in reverse, causing Mailer to burst through the rear window and drag West out before mauling him to death as the car drives away. Another twenty-eight days later, Jim recovers at a remote cottage in [Cumbria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cumbria "Cumbria"), where the infected are shown lying on the roads, dying of starvation. As a [Hawker Hunter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hawker_Hunter "Hawker Hunter") jet flies overhead, Jim, Selena, and Hannah unfurl a huge cloth banner spelling the word "HELLO". All three survivors wave at the jet, and wonder among themselves if the pilot had spotted them. ## Cast \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=2 "Edit section: Cast")\] [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/7/75/Cillian_Murphy-2014.jpg/120px-Cillian_Murphy-2014.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Cillian_Murphy-2014.jpg) [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/c/c7/Naomie_Harris_2%2C_2012.jpg/120px-Naomie_Harris_2%2C_2012.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Naomie_Harris_2,_2012.jpg) [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e7/Brendan_Gleeson.jpg/250px-Brendan_Gleeson.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Brendan_Gleeson.jpg) [Cillian Murphy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cillian_Murphy "Cillian Murphy"), [Naomie Harris](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naomie_Harris "Naomie Harris"), and [Brendan Gleeson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brendan_Gleeson "Brendan Gleeson") star in the film. - [Cillian Murphy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Cillian_Murphy "Cillian Murphy") as Jim, a bicycle courier who was previously in a coma - [Naomie Harris](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Naomie_Harris "Naomie Harris") as Selena, a survivor in London - [Brendan Gleeson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brendan_Gleeson "Brendan Gleeson") as Frank, a taxi driver - [Megan Burns](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Megan_Burns "Megan Burns") as Hannah, Frank's young daughter - [Christopher Eccleston](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christopher_Eccleston "Christopher Eccleston") as Major Henry West, the leader of the soldiers - [Noah Huntley](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Noah_Huntley "Noah Huntley") as Mark, a fellow survivor with Selena - [Stuart McQuarrie](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stuart_McQuarrie "Stuart McQuarrie") as Sergeant Farrell - [Ricci Harnett](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ricci_Harnett "Ricci Harnett") as Corporal Mitchell - [Leo Bill](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Leo_Bill "Leo Bill") as Private Jones - [Luke Mably](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Luke_Mably "Luke Mably") as Private Clifton - Junior Laniyan as Private Bell - [Ray Panthaki](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ray_Panthaki "Ray Panthaki") as Private Bedford - Sanjay Rambaruth as Private Davis - Marvin Campbell as Private Mailer Additionally, Alex Palmer, Bindu De Stoppani, and Jukka Hiltunen portray the animal liberation activists, while [David Schneider](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/David_Schneider_(actor) "David Schneider (actor)") portrays a scientist at the laboratory. Christopher Dunne and Emma Hitching appear as Jim's parents. [Toby Sedgwick](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Toby_Sedgwick "Toby Sedgwick") plays an infected priest encountered by Jim. Sedgwick would later return to the series in a behind the scenes role, acting as the movement coach for _[28 Years Later](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Years_Later "28 Years Later")_ (2025). ## Production \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=3 "Edit section: Production")\] ### Development \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=4 "Edit section: Development")\] Early influences on writer [Alex Garland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alex_Garland "Alex Garland") included the [George A. Romero](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/George_A._Romero "George A. Romero") films _[Night of the Living Dead](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Night_of_the_Living_Dead "Night of the Living Dead")_ (1968) and _[Dawn of the Dead](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Dawn_of_the_Dead_(1978_film) "Dawn of the Dead (1978 film)")_ (1978), which he loved as a child. Garland claimed to have largely forgotten about the zombie genre until he played the video game _[Resident Evil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Resident_Evil_(1996_video_game) "Resident Evil (1996 video game)")_ (1996), which reminded him how much he loved zombies after "having not really encountered zombies for quite a while".[\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-interview-7)[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:2-8) Director [Danny Boyle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Danny_Boyle "Danny Boyle") liked Garland's screenplay for a proposed zombie film, having directed [the 2000 film adaptation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Beach_(film) "The Beach (film)") of Garland's 1996 novel _[The Beach](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Beach_(novel) "The Beach (novel)")_.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:2-8) Rather than portraying zombies as supernatural beings, the film's [rage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rage_(emotion) "Rage (emotion)") virus was conceived as a biological contagion.[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-Cinema28DaysLaterProductionNotes-9) Boyle and Garland intended the virus to amplify an inherent aspect of human nature—psychological aggression—depicting rage as an uncontrollable viral force intrinsic to humanity rather than merely a physical infection.[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-Cinema28DaysLaterProductionNotes-9) This was designed to serve as a metaphor for contemporary social rage, including [road rage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Road_rage "Road rage"), [air rage](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Air_rage "Air rage"), and other everyday aggressive behaviours.[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-Cinema28DaysLaterProductionNotes-9) On the DVD [audio commentary](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Audio_commentary "Audio commentary"), Garland said the idea of Britain being under quarantine was developed during production, replacing an earlier conception of the outbreak as a worldwide contagion that included a discarded idea of infection boarding a plane to the United States; he linked the quarantine framing to contemporary UK anxieties about [BSE](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bovine_spongiform_encephalopathy "Bovine spongiform encephalopathy") (mad cow disease) and [foot-and-mouth disease](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Foot-and-mouth_disease "Foot-and-mouth disease").[\[10\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_quarantine-10) Garland described the supermarket sequence as a deliberate nod to Romero's _Dawn of the Dead_, and the chained Infected as a nod to the character "Bub" in _[Day of the Dead](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Day_of_the_Dead_(1985_film) "Day of the Dead (1985 film)")_.[\[11\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_Romero1-11)[\[12\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_Romero2-12) Garland said he was thinking of the plantation dinner sequence from _[Apocalypse Now Redux](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Apocalypse_Now_Redux "Apocalypse Now Redux")_ when writing the soldiers' dinner scene.[\[13\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_ApocalypseNow-13) Producer [Andrew Macdonald](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrew_Macdonald_(producer) "Andrew Macdonald (producer)") had access to funding from the [National Lottery](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Lottery_(United_Kingdom) "National Lottery (United Kingdom)") and [pitched](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pitch_(filmmaking) "Pitch (filmmaking)") it to [Universal Pictures](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Universal_Pictures "Universal Pictures"), who declined to support it. Budget constraints proved to be an issue, with [Christopher Eccleston](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christopher_Eccleston "Christopher Eccleston") having to take an emergency pay cut.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:2-8) In June 2001, it was reported that [Fox Searchlight Pictures](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Searchlight_Pictures "Searchlight Pictures") had picked up for co-financing as well as worldwide distribution.[\[14\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-Searchlight28Days-14) Boyle identified [John Wyndham](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Wyndham_(writer) "John Wyndham (writer)")'s 1951 novel _[The Day of the Triffids](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Day_of_the_Triffids "The Day of the Triffids")_ as Garland's original inspiration for the story.[\[15\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-Guardian2-15)[\[16\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-16)[\[17\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-17) Five months after the film was released in Europe, video game publisher [NovaLogic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/NovaLogic "NovaLogic") hosted a [graffiti](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Graffiti "Graffiti") competition in a cross-promotion with the game _[Devastation](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Devastation_(video_game) "Devastation (video game)")_ (2003). The connection was owed mainly to the similar theme of a devastated world. The prizes consisted of signed screenplay copies and posters along with DVDs.[\[18\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-18) For the Infected, Boyle took inspiration from real-life diseases, particularly [Ebola](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ebola "Ebola"), with aspects of [rabies](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rabies "Rabies").[\[19\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-19) ### Casting \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=5 "Edit section: Casting")\] On the DVD commentary, Boyle explains that with the aim of preserving the [suspension of disbelief](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Suspension_of_disbelief "Suspension of disbelief"), relatively unknown actors were cast in the film. Cillian Murphy had starred primarily in small independent films, while Naomie Harris had acted on British television as a child, and Megan Burns had only one previous film credit. However, Christopher Eccleston and [Brendan Gleeson](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brendan_Gleeson "Brendan Gleeson") were well-known [character actors](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Character_actor "Character actor"). Murphy originally auditioned for the role with a [Standard Southern British](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Standard_Southern_British "Standard Southern British") accent, as Jim was written to be English.[\[20\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:3-20)[\[21\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:4-21) However, during filming, Boyle and Murphy decided to revert to the actor's natural accent, and rewrote the character as Irish.[\[20\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:3-20)[\[21\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:4-21) [James McAvoy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/James_McAvoy "James McAvoy") auditioned for a role as an Infected.[\[22\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-22) ### Filming \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=6 "Edit section: Filming")\] [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/6a/London%2C_United_Kingdom_%28Unsplash_mfbEp8lS1hA%29.jpg/250px-London%2C_United_Kingdom_%28Unsplash_mfbEp8lS1hA%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:London,_United_Kingdom_(Unsplash_mfbEp8lS1hA).jpg) Busy areas of [Central London](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Central_London "Central London"), including [Westminster Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Westminster_Bridge "Westminster Bridge"), had to be filmed early in the morning or while the crew briefly closed streets for the film's opening sequence. The film features scenes set in normally bustling parts of London, such as [Westminster Bridge](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Westminster_Bridge "Westminster Bridge"), [Piccadilly Circus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Piccadilly_Circus "Piccadilly Circus"), [Horse Guards Parade](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Horse_Guards_Parade "Horse Guards Parade") and [Oxford Street](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Oxford_Street "Oxford Street"). To depict these locations as desolate, the film crew closed off sections of street for minutes at a time, usually on Sunday mornings. They typically had around 45 minutes after dawn to shoot the locations devoid of members of the public and traffic, with closures lasting only "90 seconds or two minutes" at a time while traffic was held back by volunteer marshals (students and friends).[\[23\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_emptyLondon-23) The majority of the film (except for the final sequence when the military plane finds Jim, Selena, and Hannah, which was shot on [35mm](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/35_mm_movie_film "35 mm movie film") film) was shot on [Canon XL1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Canon_XL1S "Canon XL1S") [digital video](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Digital_video "Digital video") (DV) cameras,[\[24\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-24)[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:2-8) which were smaller and more manoeuvrable than traditional film cameras, which would have been impractical on such brief shoots. Boyle said the early London sequences avoided "litter\[ing\] the world with corpses", instead emphasising a more symbolic emptiness and atmosphere.[\[25\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_corpses-25) The scenes of the empty [M1 motorway](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/M1_motorway "M1 motorway") were also filmed within limited periods; Boyle said police created a "rolling block" in both directions to produce a temporary corridor of empty road, giving the crew seven or eight minutes to shoot using multiple cameras.[\[26\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_M1-26) For the London scene in which Jim walks by an overturned [double-decker bus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Double-decker_bus "Double-decker bus"), the film crew placed the bus on its side and removed it when the shot was finished, all within about 15–20 minutes.[\[27\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_bus15-27)[\[28\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-28) The crew had asked [Westminster City Council](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Westminster_City_Council "Westminster City Council") for permission to place the bus outside [Downing Street](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Downing_Street "Downing Street"), which was denied; when they arrived there at 4am and nobody from the council was present, they placed the bus there anyway.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:2-8) The [September 11 attacks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/September_11_attacks "September 11 attacks") took place during filming.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:2-8) Boyle notes the parallel between the "missing persons" flyers seen at the beginning of the film and similar flyers posted in New York City in the wake of the attacks. Boyle said his crew probably would not have been granted permission to close off [Whitehall](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Whitehall "Whitehall") for filming after the terrorist attacks, citing the production's ability to stage an overturned bus there before the attacks.[\[27\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_bus15-27)[\[29\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-29) The mansion used in the film was [Trafalgar Park](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Trafalgar_House_(Wiltshire) "Trafalgar House (Wiltshire)") near [Salisbury](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Salisbury "Salisbury").[\[30\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-30)[\[31\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-31) The old ruins used as the setting for an idyllic interlude in their journey to Manchester were those of [Waverley Abbey](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waverley_Abbey "Waverley Abbey"), Surrey. The end scenes of the film with Jim, Selena and Hannah living in a rural cottage were filmed around [Ennerdale](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ennerdale_Water "Ennerdale Water") in Cumbria.[\[32\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-Cumbria-32) The production team hired an [optometrist](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Optometrist "Optometrist") to supervise with the red [contact lenses](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Contact_lens "Contact lens") needed for cast members playing the infected.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:2-8) Boyle said the supermarket scene was filmed overnight at a Budgens on Tottenham Court Road using the store's overhead lighting; to save time, the crew avoided neon where possible and had the visual-effects house Clear paint out remaining neon in post-production.[\[33\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_supermarket-33) Boyle said some pick-ups were shot by cinematographer Alwin Küchler because Anthony Dod Mantle was unavailable.[\[34\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_pickups-34) Boyle said the film initially ended with Jim being shot, which marked the end of the first budget; after showing the cut to Fox, the production received additional funding and shot the final sequence months later.[\[35\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_budget-35) After several different pitches for a new ending, with the original ending which featured Jim's death having [tested](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Test_screening "Test screening") badly with audiences, the studio approved the ending eventually used. The crew arranged for a real jet to fly overhead for them to film, as this was cheaper than approximately £70,000 for a [computer-generated](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Computer-generated_imagery "Computer-generated imagery") one.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:2-8) Boyle said Fox funded the Lake District ending, and that the final rural sequence was shot on 35mm to make the film "feel like \[it\] had opened up".[\[36\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_35mm-36) Boyle said the overflight was filmed with an RAF jet standing in for the Finnish Air Force, and that the markings could not be changed.[\[37\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-DVDcom_RAFjet-37) ### Alternative endings \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=7 "Edit section: Alternative endings")\] The DVD extras include three alternative endings, all of which conclude with Jim dying. The first alternate ending involves Jim dying of his gunshot wounds in a hospital despite Selena and Hannah's efforts to save him, concluding with the two of them leaving the hospital to an unknown fate.[\[38\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:1-38) The US cinematic release included this ending after the film's credits in response to intense online debates over whether or not it was a more appropriate conclusion than the official ending.[\[39\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-39) In the second, Jim dreams of the accident that left him in a coma as he dies from his gunshot wounds.[\[40\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-40) The third, a more radical departure, was presented only in [storyboards](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Storyboard "Storyboard"); instead of Frank being killed by soldiers after being infected, the other survivors tie him up and discover a research laboratory at the blockade, where Jim undergoes a full [blood transfusion](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blood_transfusion "Blood transfusion") in order to save Frank, at the cost of his own life. This ending was never filmed due to Boyle and Garland agreeing that it lacked plausibility.[\[38\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:1-38) ## Music \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=8 "Edit section: Music")\] Main article: [28 Days Later: The Soundtrack Album](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later:_The_Soundtrack_Album "28 Days Later: The Soundtrack Album") ![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/Gnome-mime-sound-openclipart.svg/60px-Gnome-mime-sound-openclipart.svg.png) [John Murphy – "In the House - In a Heartbeat"](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:In_The_House_-_In_A_Heartbeat.ogg "File:In The House - In A Heartbeat.ogg") [Play audio](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:In_The_House_-_In_A_Heartbeat.ogg "Play audio") Duration: 30 seconds.0:30 Music from the 2002 film _28 Days Later_. * * * _Problems playing this file? See [media help](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Help:Media "Help:Media")._ The film's score was composed by [John Murphy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/John_Murphy_(composer) "John Murphy (composer)"), featuring [electric guitar](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electric_guitar "Electric guitar") and atmospheric [electronic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Electronic_music "Electronic music") production. It also features tracks from [Brian Eno](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Brian_Eno "Brian Eno"), [Grandaddy](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Grandaddy "Grandaddy") and [Blue States](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blue_States_(band) "Blue States (band)"),[\[41\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-41) and was released in a combined score and song compilation in 2003. A heavily edited version of the track " [East Hastings](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/East_Hastings_(song) "East Hastings (song)")" by the [post-rock](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Post-rock "Post-rock") band [Godspeed You! Black Emperor](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Godspeed_You!_Black_Emperor "Godspeed You! Black Emperor") appears in the film, but the track is excluded from the soundtrack, because Boyle could only obtain the rights to use it in the film.[\[42\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-Guardian-42) _28 Days Later: The Soundtrack Album_ was released on 17 June 2003. A modified version of the track "In the House - In a Heartbeat" was used as the character Big Daddy's theme in the 2010 film _[Kick-Ass](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kick-Ass_(film) "Kick-Ass (film)")_.[\[43\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-Y-43) The same song was played in the 2012 advertisement campaign of [Louis Vuitton](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Louis_Vuitton "Louis Vuitton"), "L'Invitation au voyage".[\[44\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-44) In 2019, the song was remixed and used in the video game _[Metro Exodus](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metro_Exodus "Metro Exodus")_.[\[45\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-45) ## Release and reception \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=9 "Edit section: Release and reception")\] ### Box office \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=10 "Edit section: Box office")\] _28 Days Later_ was a considerable success at the box office and became highly profitable on a budget of about £5 million. In the UK, it took in £6.1 million, while in the US, it became a surprise hit, taking over $45 million despite a limited release at fewer than 1,500 screens across the country.[\[1\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-mojo-1) The film garnered over $82.8 million worldwide.[\[46\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-46)[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-mojo2-3) [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/6/60/28DL_V002_20CS_SM.jpg/250px-28DL_V002_20CS_SM.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:28DL_V002_20CS_SM.jpg) Retrospective screening poster variation ### Critical reception \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=11 "Edit section: Critical reception")\] Critical views of the film were positive. On the film review aggregator [Rotten Tomatoes](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rotten_Tomatoes "Rotten Tomatoes"), 87% of 237 critics' reviews gave _28 Days Later_ a positive review, with an average rating of 7.40/10. The site's consensus reads: "Kinetically directed by Danny Boyle, _28 Days Later_ is both a terrifying zombie movie and a sharp political allegory."[\[47\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-47) On [Metacritic](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Metacritic "Metacritic"), the film received a rating of 73 out of 100 based on 39 reviews, indicating "generally favourable reviews".[\[48\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-48) _[Bravo](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bravo_(American_TV_network) "Bravo (American TV network)")_ awarded it the 100th spot on their list of "The 100 Scariest Movie Moments" in a four-episode 2004 television series. The commentators explained that making the zombies move fast for the first time was a bright and effective idea.[\[49\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-49)[\[50\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-50) In 2007, _[Stylus Magazine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Stylus_Magazine "Stylus Magazine")_ named it the second-best [zombie movie](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_zombie_films "List of zombie films") of all time.[\[51\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-51) The film also ranked at number 456 in [_Empire_](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Empire_(film_magazine) "Empire (film magazine)")'s 2008 list of the 500 greatest movies of all time.[\[52\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-52) [Bloody Disgusting](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Bloody_Disgusting "Bloody Disgusting") ranked the film seventh in their list of the Top 20 Horror Films of the Decade, with the article saying "Zombie movie? Political allegory? Humanist drama? _28 Days Later_ is all of those things and more—a genuine work of art by a director at the top of his game. What's so amazing about the film is the way it so expertly balances scenes of white-knuckled, hell-for-leather horror with moments of intimate beauty."[\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-Bloody-Disgusting-6) In 2017, a poll of 150 actors, directors, writers, producers and critics for _[Time Out](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Time_Out_(magazine) "Time Out (magazine)")_ magazine ranked it the [97th-best British film ever](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Time_Out_100_best_British_films "Time Out 100 best British films").[\[53\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-53) Rotten Tomatoes lists the film on its 100 Best Zombie Movies, Ranked by Tomatometer.[\[54\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-54) In July 2025, it was one of the films voted for the "Readers' Choice" edition of _[The New York Times](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_New_York_Times "The New York Times")_' list of "The 100 Best Movies of the 21st Century", finishing at number 156.[\[55\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-55) That same month, it ranked number 53 on _[Rolling Stone](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")_'s list of "The 100 Best Movies of the 21st Century,"[\[56\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-56) and _[The Hollywood Reporter](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Hollywood_Reporter "The Hollywood Reporter")_ ranked the film at number six on its list of the "25 Best Horror Movies of the 21st Century."[\[57\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-57) ### Accolades \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=12 "Edit section: Accolades")\] - Best Horror Film (2003 US Academy of Science Fiction, Fantasy & Horror Films – Saturn Award)[\[58\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-58) - Best British Film (Empire Award)[\[59\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-59) - Danny Boyle (Grand Prize of European Fantasy Film in Silver)[\[60\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:0-60) - Best Director – Danny Boyle (International Fantasy Film Award)[\[61\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-61) - Best International Film – Danny Boyle ( [Narcisse Award](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neuch%C3%A2tel_International_Fantastic_Film_Festival "Neuchâtel International Fantastic Film Festival"))[\[60\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:0-60) - Best Breakthrough Performance – Naomie Harris (Black Reel)[\[60\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:0-60) - Best Cinematographer – Anthony Dod Mantle (European Film Award)[\[60\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-:0-60) ### Home media \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=13 "Edit section: Home media")\] _28 Days Later_ was released on DVD and VHS in Europe on May 16, 2003[\[62\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-62) and in North America on October 21, 2003,[\[63\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-63) both by [20th Century Fox Home Entertainment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/20th_Century_Home_Entertainment "20th Century Home Entertainment"). DVD bonus features included a three alternate endings, an audio commentary, a making-of featurette, a [Jacknife Lee](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jacknife_Lee "Jacknife Lee") music video, deleted scenes, photo galleries, and trailers. A Blu-ray Disc version was released on October 9, 2007[\[64\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-64) alongside _[28 Weeks Later](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Weeks_Later "28 Weeks Later"),_ which included the DVD bonus features and added an additional alternate ending. The film was notorious for being largely unavailable to purchase on home video, attributed to out-of-print copies and expensive secondhand prices of the film on [Blu-ray](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Blu-ray "Blu-ray"), [DVD](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/DVD "DVD") and [VHS](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/VHS "VHS"), as well as its unavailability on digital.[\[65\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-65) In February 2024, it was revealed that producer [Andrew MacDonald](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrew_MacDonald_(producer) "Andrew MacDonald (producer)") had bought back the rights to the film from [Disney](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/The_Walt_Disney_Company "The Walt Disney Company") (parent company of [Searchlight Pictures](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Searchlight_Pictures "Searchlight Pictures")), and promptly sold it to [Sony Pictures Releasing](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sony_Pictures_Releasing "Sony Pictures Releasing") through its [Columbia Pictures](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Columbia_Pictures "Columbia Pictures") label, along with the rights to the _28 Years Later_ trilogy ( _[28 Years Later](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Years_Later "28 Years Later")_, _[28 Years Later: The Bone Temple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Years_Later:_The_Bone_Temple "28 Years Later: The Bone Temple")_, and an unnamed third movie), though _28 Weeks Later,_ in which 20th Century Fox was a co-producer, remained with Disney.[\[66\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-puck-66)[\[67\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-fangoria-67) In December 2024, following a largely favorable response from the public to the trailer for _28 Years Later_, Sony announced that it would be making _28 Days Later_ available for purchase on digital and rental platforms from December 18.[\[68\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-68) The following year, [Sony Pictures Home Entertainment](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Sony_Pictures_Home_Entertainment "Sony Pictures Home Entertainment") announced a 4K remaster of the film to be released with a [steelbook](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steelbook "Steelbook") cover,[\[69\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-69) which was later announced to be scheduled for a September 1, 2026 release.[\[70\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-70) ## Legacy and cultural impact \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=14 "Edit in _28 Days Later_. _28 Days Later_ had an impact on subsequent [horror films](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Horror_film "Horror film"),[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-hollywoodreporter-5) and is credited with starting a revival for the [zombie genre](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Zombie_film "Zombie film"),[\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-interview-7)[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-hollywoodreporter-5)[\[71\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-revival-71) along with the _[Resident Evil](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Resident_Evil "Resident Evil")_ Am Legend](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/I_Am_Legend_(film) "I Am Legend (film)")_ (2007),[\[73\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-ew-73) _[Planet XX](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Treehouse_of_Horror_XX "Treehouse of Horror XX")" (2009).[\[79\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-79) The zombie revival trend lasted for more In 2021, Megan Burns said of the film, "When I joined the cast of _28 Days Later_ I had no idea of how big a cultural impact it would have and what a game-changer it would be to the 'zombie' genre. Even now after all these years, (or perhaps especially now with the current situation), people want to talk about the film and that's incredible."[\[84\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-84) [Christopher Nolan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Christopher_Nolan "Christopher Nolan") said he cast Murphy as [Scarecrow](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Scarecrow_(DC_Comics) "Scarecrow (DC Comics)") in _[Batman Begins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Batman_Begins "Batman Begins")_ (2005) after being impressed by his performance in _28 Days Later_.[\[85\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-85) ## Subsequent media \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=15 "Edit section: Subsequent media")\] ### Sequels \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=16 "Edit section: Sequels")\] Main article: [_28 Days Later_ (film series)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later_(film_series) "28 Days Later (film series)") A sequel, _[28 Weeks Later](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Weeks_Later "28 Weeks Later")_, was released on 11 May 2007.[\[86\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-86) Danny Boyle and [Alex Garland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alex_Garland "Alex Garland") took producing roles alongside [Andrew Macdonald](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Andrew_Macdonald_(producer) "Andrew Macdonald (producer)"). The plot revolves around the arrival of American troops about seven months after the incidents in the original film, attempting to restore order and revitalise a nearly desolate Britain. The cast includes [Robert Carlyle](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robert_Carlyle "Robert Carlyle"), [Rose Byrne](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Rose_Byrne "Rose Byrne"), [Jeremy Renner](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Jeremy_Renner "Jeremy Renner"), [Imogen Poots](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Imogen_Poots "Imogen Poots"), [Harold Perrineau](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Harold_Perrineau "Harold Perrineau"), [Catherine McCormack](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Catherine_McCormack "Catherine McCormack"), Mackintosh Muggleton and [Idris Elba](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Idris_Elba "Idris Elba"). A third film, _[28 Years Later](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Years_Later "28 Years Later")_, was released on 20 June 2025, its development having been announced in January 2024. Danny Boyle had said in March 2007 that he would be interested in making a third film in the series, _28 Months Later_.[\[87\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-87) In 2019, he said "Alex Garland and I have a wonderful idea for the third part".[\[88\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-88) _28 Years Later_ is considered the first instalment in a potential trilogy, with Boyle directing the first film and Garland writing all three. With a planned $75 million budget per instalment, the duo will also produce the trilogy alongside the original producer Andrew Macdonald and former Fox Searchlight Pictures head [Peter Rice](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Peter_Rice_(executive) "Peter Rice (executive)").[\[89\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-89) The fourth film, _[28 Years Later: The Bone Temple](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Years_Later:_The_Bone_Temple "28 Years Later: The Bone Temple")_, was directed by [Nia DaCosta](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nia_DaCosta "Nia DaCosta") and filmed back-to-back with its predecessor. It premiered in the United Kingdom on 13 January 2026 as part of a double feature with _28 Years Later_, followed by individual releases in the UK on 14 January and the United States on 16 January. The plot continues the narrative of the previous film and reintroduces the character portrayed by Cillian Murphy from _28 Days Later_,[\[90\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-90) setting up the fifth instalment of the franchise.[\[91\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-91) Following the success of _28 Years Later_ and _The Bone Temple_, Sony confirmed that the third and final film of the planned _28 Years Later_ trilogy is in development, with Danny Boyle returning as director and Alex Garland writing.[\[92\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-92) ### Comic books \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=28_Days_Later&action=edit§ion=17 "Edit section: Comic books")\] In 2007, [Fox Atomic Comics](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fox_Atomic "Fox Atomic"), in association with [HarperCollins](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/HarperCollins "HarperCollins"), published _[28 Days Later: The Aftermath](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later:_The_Aftermath "28 Days Later: The Aftermath")_, a [graphic novel](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Graphic_novel "Graphic novel") written by [Steve Niles](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Steve_Niles "Steve Niles"). The work serves as a tie-in to the films, depicting events surrounding the Rage virus outbreak and providing background that connects _28 Days Later_ with its sequel _[28 Weeks Later](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Weeks_Later "28 Weeks Later")_.[\[93\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later#cite_note-93) A separate monthly comic book series, also titled _[28 Days Later](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/28_Days_Later_(comics) "28 Days Later (comics)")_, was launched in 2009 by [Boom! Studios](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Boom!_Studios "Boom! Studios") following the closure of Fox Atomic.
